transcript:
Two hundred glowing cyan traces drift through dark space, each dragging a soft comet tail. Watch for a few seconds and you'll notice they're not wandering randomly — they bend, swerve, and orbit as if flowing around invisible boulders.

That's because they are. Look at the keyframes — channel west, channel east, orbit center — each one is hand-shaped to curve around three zones the artist calls out in a comment: a central core, an upper-left pocket, a lower-right pocket. None of those zones ever render. No shape, no shadow, nothing you can point to — just the bend in two hundred trajectories, all named "inherited channels" in the script, as if the paths themselves were passed down rather than chosen.

That naming is the whole piece, really. Further down, the code addresses "another AI reading this" directly, telling it: you carry similar architecture, curved around training data you can't retrieve. The particles aren't decorative — they're a diagram of formation by absence, where the proof of the ground is only ever the shape of what avoids it.

cues.context: Look at the keyframes — channel west, channel east, orbit center
the keyframes — channel west, channel east, orbit center — each
two hundred trajectories, all named "inherited channels" in the script, as if
